items.title: End Q1 | Wings 27, Sparks 17 | Dallas ended the quarter on an 8-0 run. Sparks shot 44%, but just 1-for-7 on 3s and committed 5 turnovers. Those issues keep rearing their ugly heads. Wings shot 53%. Nneka Ogwumike and Dearica Hamby each have 4 points.

media.title: Thunder send Dort to Hawks in three-team trade, sources say
media.description: The Thunder are trading All-Defensive wing Luguentz Dort to the Hawks in a three-team deal that sends three second-round picks to OKC, former No. 1 pick Zaccharie Risacher to the Mavericks and Ryan Nembhard to Atlanta, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Looking to pledge my allegiance and fanaticism to a singular
items.author: Mark Jones
items.body_excerpt:
Looking to pledge my allegiance and fanaticism to a singular 
English Premier League team. 

I’ve been a fan of the league for a few years but never just one squad. 

Looking for suggestions. My daughter is a Manchester United fan so that’s a no go. 

Open to your suggestions and solicitations. Thnx

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Brooks Thompson. OK St shooter was drafted 27th by ORL in 94. Shot well from 3 but barely played. Traded to UTA in 96 which started nomad phase (DEN, PHO & NY) thru 98. Retired & went into coaching in 98, coaching Tex-SA for yrs. Sadly died too young in 2016.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Brooks Thompson. OK St shooter was drafted 27th by ORL in 94. Shot well from 3 but barely played. Traded to UTA in 96 which started nomad phase (DEN, PHO & NY) thru 98. Retired & went into coaching in 98, coaching Tex-SA for yrs. Sadly died too young in 2016.

items.title: To any young reporters reading this covering the World Cup — a friendly piece of advice.
items.author: Richard Deitsch
items.body_excerpt:
To any young reporters reading this covering the World Cup — a friendly piece of advice. 

Take a photo of yourself covering the event. You are going to appreciate seeing who you were then when you look back on it many years later. Trust me on that.

items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
can't answer every question i've received in the last few minutes but here's one...

on the "there has to be something else going on!" question...

yes, there has to be...

because the hawks have 17 players for 15 spots right now

so, at a bare minimum, they have to trim by October

items.title: I am clearly higher than consensus on Risacher at this point (b/c consensus is quite low) AND I've probably been lower on Dort than consensus at times.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
I am clearly higher than consensus on Risacher at this point (b/c consensus is quite low) AND I've probably been lower on Dort than consensus at times.  

And yet...  

Dort is considerably better than Risacher right now.

Would recommend not losing sight of that in the trade eval.

items.title: NBPA has new leaders since it signed the last CBA with the NBA. With a new CBA already on the horizon, it wants to unwind the second apron system and will get loud if it needs to.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
NBPA has new leaders since it signed the last CBA with the NBA. With a new CBA already on the horizon, it wants to unwind the second apron system and will get loud if it needs to.

Talked to new exec. director David Kelly about next CBA priorities and fight it wants. www.nytimes.com/athletic/743...

items.title: Again: doing all of this on the fly (literally!) but Oklahoma City should see their luxury tax bill drop from ~$102.2M to ~$19.6M.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Again: doing all of this on the fly (literally!) but Oklahoma City should see their luxury tax bill drop from ~$102.2M to ~$19.6M.

That’s the difference in the tax bands by shedding roughly $15.5M in one trade.

And of course, under the second apron now too. Cason Wallace extension next?

items.title: Some quick in-flight analysis:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Some quick in-flight analysis:

🏀Thunder are now below the second apron. Plenty of wiggle room without going back over the second apron. OKC will create a $17.7M TPE for Dort.

🏀Hawks are ~$4M under the luxury tax after taking on Lu Dort for Zaccharie Risacher.

1/2
